typography.mono_stack: ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Menlo, Monaco, Consolas, monospace
typography.headings: Inter font with font-medium to font-bold weight. Avoid generic center alignment.
typography.section_labels: Uppercase 10-11px with tracking-[0.22em] (e.g. text-[10px] uppercase tracking-[0.22em] font-semibold text-slate-400)
typography.body: 12-14px (text-xs to text-sm) for standard text. Dense IDE-like typography.
typography.numeric_params: Monospaced font at 10px or 12px (text-[10px] font-mono or text-xs font-mono) for precise alignment.

colors.secondary_accent: cyan-400 (#22d3ee) for info, emerald-400 (#34d399) for success
colors.text: slate-200 for primary text, slate-400 for secondary, slate-500 for tertiary
prohibited: generic vibrant colors that break the professional tool aesthetic
pure transparent backgrounds behind text or dropdowns

visual_enhancers.borders: 1px crisp borders (border-slate-800) between all logical panels to create a structural, native application feel.
visual_enhancers.background_texture: Subtle grid backgrounds (radial-gradient(#1e293b 1px, transparent 1px)) in 3D viewport areas to imply 3D space.
visual_enhancers.shadows: Minimal ambient shadows except for primary CTA buttons (e.g. shadow-lg shadow-orange-500/20) and floating toolbars.

layouts.app_shell: Full height (h-screen flex flex-col overflow-hidden) with a fixed header and flexible content area (flex-1 overflow-hidden).
layouts.ai_studio_surface: Embedded inside the workspace shell. Retains top app header. Displays an internal navigation header specifically for AI modes with Back to Workspace chip.
layouts.columns: Dense, rigid-width column layout (e.g. w-72 or w-[340px] for config panels, flex-1 for 3D viewports).

motion.hover_states: Subtle transition-colors on buttons and panels (hover:bg-slate-800, hover:text-white).
motion.buttons: Slight brightness change or inner border reveal, no excessive scaling.
motion.accessibility: Respect prefers-reduced-motion. Ensure visual changes on focus (focus:outline-none focus:border-orange-500 or focus:ring-1 focus:ring-orange-500).

accessibility.contrast: Ensure text meets WCAG AA (at least 4.5:1 for normal text). Slate-400 on Slate-900 is compliant.
accessibility.attributes: All interactive and key informational elements MUST include a data-testid attribute to facilitate robust automated testing.
accessibility.focus: Visible focus states required on inputs, textareas, and interactive tabs.

media.images: Use realistic references via Unsplash. Grayscale filtering (mix-blend modes) often used for bas-relief height map visualization.
media.icons: Lucide React (lucide-react) exclusively. Ensure crisp rendering at small sizes (w-3.5 h-3.5 or w-4 h-4).

component_patterns.slider: Minimalist range input with accent color (accent-slate-500). Numeric values beside them wrapped in monospaced badges.
component_patterns.toggle: Custom styled checkbox acting as a pill switch (emerald-500 when active, slate-800 when inactive) built with purely CSS sibling selectors.
component_patterns.tabs: Segmented controls with dark backgrounds (bg-slate-950 border-slate-800) and active items highlighted via background (bg-slate-800) and text color (text-white).
component_patterns.image_upload: Dashed border dropzone with icon and helper text, dark inset background.

instructions_to_main_agent: Ensure all merged UI looks like a native subsection of a one-page app, not a standalone website.
Use exact color tokens specified. Do NOT introduce new hues like purple or generic Tailwind blue.
Maintain high density (IDE style): small text (10-12px) for labels, tight padding (p-3 to p-5) for panels.
When building the components, map them closely to the HTML mocks provided in /app/frontend/public/design-mocks/.
Ensure the top header stays untouched structurally except for the active tab highlighting.
Include the 'Back to Workspace' chip and Trial Banner as shown in the mocks.
UNIVERSAL GUIDELINES FOR MAIN AGENT: You tend to converge toward generic, 'on distribution' outputs. In frontend design, this creates what users call the 'AI slop' aesthetic. Avoid this: make creative, distinctive frontends that surprise and delight.
Dark colors look good when used independently without gradients. DO NOT use dark colors as gradients.
Don't make generic centered layouts, simplistic gradients, and uniform styling.
Create depth through layered design elements with z-index hierarchy and subtle inner shadows.
Use glass-morphism effects with backdrop filters (backdrop-blur bg-slate-900/80) where necessary (e.g. floating toolbars), but prefer solid crisp panels for standard layout structures.

You MUST NOT apply universal transition (e.g. transition: all). This results in breaking transforms. Always add transitions for specific properties (transition-colors, transition-opacity, transition-transform).
You MUST NOT center align the app container. This disrupts the human natural reading flow of text.
Always use modern button styles like sharp or slightly rounded corners with interaction animations & color relevant to the app style.
NEVER use AI assistant Emoji characters for icons. Use Lucide icons.
All interactive elements MUST include data-testid.
